title: Valiyakoikkal Temple
tagline: Valiyakoikkal Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Valiakoikkal Temple is the family temple of the Pandalam Royal Family.
overview.description: Valiakoikkal Temple is the family temple of the Pandalam Royal Family. The temple is located at Pandalam in Pathanamthitta District, Kerala, in the peninsular India. It is situated within the Pandalam Palace premises. The main deity is Ayyappan.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kozhencherry East
topPlaces.description: Kozhencherry East, is a village in Pathanamthitta district of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Naranganam
topPlaces.description: Naranganam is a village in Pathanamthitta district in the state of Kerala, India. It is home to Madathumpadi Devi Temple.

topPlaces.name: Chekkulam
topPlaces.description: Chekkulam is a small place in Kozhencherry, Pathanamthitta District, Kerala State, India.

topPlaces.name: Kattoor, Pathanamthitta
topPlaces.description: Kattoor is a village near Kozhencherry on the southern side of Pamba river on the way from Kozhencherry to Ranni in Cherukole Panchayath in Pathanamthitta District which is part of the state of Kerala, India. Kattoor is situated 5 km east of Kozhencherry town and 9 km west of Ranni town.

topPlaces.name: Keekozhur
topPlaces.description: Keekozhur, also spelt as Keekozhoor, is a small village on the side of Pamba River, situated 4 km from Ranni and 9 km from Kozhencherry. It is in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Thekkemala
topPlaces.description: Thekkemala is a small sub-village of Kozhencherry in Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kozhencherry
topPlaces.description: Kozhencherry is a census town in Pathanamthitta district of South Central Kerala in Kerala state, India. As of 2011 census, the population was 12,021 of which 5,594 are males while 6,427 are females.

topPlaces.name: Maramon Convention
topPlaces.description: The Maramon Convention, is the largest Christian convention in Asia, held at Maramon, Kozhencherry, Pathanamthitta, Kerala, India annually in February on the vast sand-bed of the Pampa River next to the Kozhencherry Bridge. It is organised by Mar Thoma Evangelistic Association, the missionary wing of the Malankara Mar Thoma Syrian Church.

items.note: Kozhencherry East, is a village in Pathanamthitta district of Kerala, India.
Naranganam is a village in Pathanamthitta district in the state of Kerala, India. It is home to Madathumpadi Devi Temple.
Chekkulam is a small place in Kozhencherry, Pathanamthitta District, Kerala State, India.

Kattoor is a village near Kozhencherry on the southern side of Pamba river on the way from Kozhencherry to Ranni in Cherukole Panchayath in Pathanamthitta District which is part of the state of Kerala, India. Kattoor is situated 5 km east of Kozhencherry town and 9 km west of Ranni town.
Keekozhur, also spelt as Keekozhoor, is a small village on the side of Pamba River, situated 4 km from Ranni and 9 km from Kozhencherry. It is in the state of Kerala, India.
Thekkemala is a small sub-village of Kozhencherry in Pathanamthitta district, Kerala, India.
Kozhencherry is a census town in Pathanamthitta district of South Central Kerala in Kerala state, India. As of 2011 census, the population was 12,021 of which 5,594 are males while 6,427 are females.

The Maramon Convention, is the largest Christian convention in Asia, held at Maramon, Kozhencherry, Pathanamthitta, Kerala, India annually in February on the vast sand-bed of the Pampa River next to the Kozhencherry Bridge. It is organised by Mar Thoma Evangelistic Association, the missionary wing of the Malankara Mar Thoma Syrian Church.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Valiyakoikkal Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Valiyakoikkal Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Valiyakoikkal Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (95 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (87 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Valiyakoikkal Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Valiyakoikkal Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Valiyakoikkal Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Valiyakoikkal Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Valiyakoikkal Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kozhencherry East, Naranganam, Chekkulam, Kattoor, Pathanamthitta, Keekozhur.
